name: Servo Component
description: The `servo` component allows you to use servo motors with ESPHome. Servos are motor controllers that contain all the electronics necessary for driving the motor and provide a simple PWM interface to control the motor.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The ID of this servo so that it can be controlled.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: The ID of the [output component](https://esphome.io/components/output/) to use for this servo.

config_entries.label: Auto Detach Time
config_entries.description: The time after reaching the target value when the servo will be detached, if set to zero, servo will not be detached. Defaults to `0s`.

config_entries.label: Idle Level
config_entries.description: The PWM duty cycle the idle value (0%) will map to. This is also the state of the servo at startup. Defaults to `7.5%`.

config_entries.label: Max Level
config_entries.description: The PWM duty cycle the maximum value (100%) will map to. Defaults to `12.0%`.

config_entries.label: Min Level
config_entries.description: The PWM duty cycle the minimum value (-100%) will map to. Defaults to `3%`.

config_entries.label: Restore
config_entries.description: Whether to restore the state of the servo motor at startup. This is useful if you have an absolute servo motor and it goes back to its 0 position at startup. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Transition Length
config_entries.description: The time needed for a full movement (-1.0 to 1.0). This will effectively limit the speed of the servo, the larger the value, the slowest the servo will move. Defaults to `0s`. This can slow down the servo to avoid loud noises or just make the movement not jerking.
